assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment: Session-Level Embeddings for Enterprise Churn Prediction

The design is well-structured with temporal split, stratification by plan tier and tenure, and appropriate use of PR-AUC for imbalanced classes. The primary concern is the label definition.

The hypothesis is about predicting commercial churn — non-renewal, downgrade, seat reduction — events with direct revenue impact. The label definition adds 'sustained near-zero engagement state' as a positive class. Behavioral disengagement is a different construct from commercial churn. An enterprise account can be in near-zero engagement (budget freeze, team restructuring) without canceling; an engaged account can cancel suddenly when a competitor offers better pricing. Including near-zero engagement as a positive class redefines the prediction target from 'will this account have a commercial churn event' to 'will this account disengage or churn.' This creates scope mismatch: the model's performance on the hybrid label cannot be used to conclude that session embeddings improve commercial churn prediction. Session embeddings are particularly well-suited to detecting near-zero engagement (directly visible in event logs), so any model advantage may reflect disengagement detection rather than commercial churn prediction.
